sections.content:
The pair moved from cols 16-17/18-21 to cols 20-21/22-25. The white region expanded from cols 0-15 to cols 0-19. So the white region CAN expand beyond col 15! It was limited before, but now it expanded to col 19.
So the pattern is:
- Each click on bottom blue moves the pair 4 cols right and expands the white region by 4 cols.
- The white region can expand beyond col 15.
The pair is now at cols 20-21/22-25. The target is at cols 28-29. The pair needs to move 8 more cols right (2 more steps).
Let me click the bottom blue 2 more times.

sections.content:

- Pair at cols 20-21/22-25, target at cols 28-29.
- White region expanded to cols 0-19 (was limited before but can expand further).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Click bottom blue 2 more times to reach target
predict(expect_change=True, note='Moving pair right 2 steps from cols 20-21 to cols 28-29')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2}])

print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("\n=== PAIR AND TARGET ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'c') and n['pixels'] < 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} pixels={n['pixels']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
else:
    print("Level not yet complete")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
